title: suaeda helenae
intro: Suaeda helenae, also known as Helen's seablite or Helen's glasswort, is a species of halophytic (salt-tolerant) flowering plant in the family Amaranthaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Suaeda helenae include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and protect against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and immune function.
